title: phlox pilosa subsp. riparia
intro: Phlox pilosa subsp. riparia, also known as wild phlox or fragrant phlox, is a type of flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its pink to purple fragrant flowers and edible foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Phlox pilosa subsp. riparia include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Phlox pilosa subsp. riparia can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the flowers and leaves in tinctures, salves, and ointments.
